title: Why not let the counter run down
display_title: Why not let the counter run down
wikitext:
[]: Why not let the counter run down?             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Nov 18, 2010 4:32 pm Something I remain unclear on: When the Eva MP series attacked Asuka in EoE, she had three and a half minutes left on the clock. Her umbilical was severed, and both SEELE and the JSSDF knew this. Why bother with the Eva series? Why engage her at all instead of just letting her timer run out, pulling her out of Unit 02 and shooting her in the head? I can't see how they had anything to prove at that point, so the engagement seems pointless past making sport of her. Am I missing something obvious, or is that exactly why they did it?

[]: Re: Why not let the counter run down?             Quote Post by Azathoth » Thu Nov 18, 2010 4:38 pm Bagheera wrote: Something I remain unclear on: When the Eva MP series attacked Asuka in EoE, she had three and a half minutes left on the clock. Her umbilical was severed, and both SEELE and the JSSDF knew this. Why bother with the Eva series? Why engage her at all instead of just letting her timer run out, pulling her out of Unit 02 and shooting her in the head? I can't see how they had anything to prove to that point, so I can't see the point past making sport of her. Am I missing something obvious, or is that exactly why they did it?

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Thu Nov 18, 2010 4:40 pm The MP Evas were supposed to start the ceremony there -- they can have some fun before it starts as well. And the scene looks more cinematically appealing than JSSDF soldiers killing Asuka. Hey! even Anno has to make money.

[]: Re: Why not let the counter run down?             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Nov 18, 2010 5:05 pm Azathoth wrote: Evas have a bad habit of not turning off all the way when the battery runs out, especially if they get fired up. And look at it this way: the battery running out didn't stop Unit 02 ( "I'll kill you..." ), getting nailed to the ground, dismembered and eaten did. And nobody in any position of power wants Unit 02 interfering with what's about to happen - hell, Yui specifically waits until she's received confirmation that Unit 02 and Asuka are out of the way before she does anything.

[]: Re: Why not let the counter run down?             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Nov 18, 2010 5:09 pm Azathoth wrote: Evas have a bad habit of not turning off all the way when the battery runs out, especially if they get fired up.

[]: Re: Why not let the counter run down?             Quote Post by Azathoth » Thu Nov 18, 2010 5:13 pm Bagheera wrote: Note, though, that Unit 02 didn't go berserk until they started eating it. If the counter had run down and they'd left it alone there's no reason to think it would have reactivated.

[]: Re: Why not let the counter run down?             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Nov 18, 2010 7:40 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: That is a logical explanation. But still, you could ask why they don't just let her timer run out, just to be on the safe side.

[]: Re: Why not let the counter run down?             Quote Post by Azathoth » Thu Nov 18, 2010 8:20 pm Bagheera wrote: I admit simple arrogance is a plausible explanation. Seems awfully risky given that one blown up dummy plug or core could fuck up the whole enterprise, though.

[]: Re: Why not let the counter run down?             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Nov 18, 2010 8:36 pm Azathoth wrote: If we consider the power of S2 engines to be additive, Seele only technically need two MP Evas to subdue Unit 01. They're already bitching about how they don't have as many as they want (12, I assume), I doubt one more or less would have made much of a difference to them.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Nov 19, 2010 3:38 pm Because the faster they can destroy Unit 02, the faster they can do their damn job.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Nov 19, 2010 4:35 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Because the faster they can destroy Unit 02, the faster they can do their damn job.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Fri Nov 19, 2010 5:39 pm From a purely strategical viewpoint, using the JSSDF forces until the internal battery depletes is wasteful of human resources and vehicles. They can't damage the Evangelion, so continuing the attack is futile. The only thing that can come near it is another Evangelion. "Fight fire with fire", as they say.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Nov 19, 2010 6:01 pm Allemann wrote: From a purely strategical viewpoint, using the JSSDF forces until the internal battery depletes is wasteful of human resources and vehicles. They can't damage the Evangelion, so continuing the attack is futile. The only thing that can come near it is another Evangelion. "Fight fire with fire", as they say.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Fri Nov 19, 2010 6:15 pm Bagheera wrote: ...or just retreat once you sever the cable.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Nov 19, 2010 6:28 pm Allemann wrote: Retreat where? From the JSSDF's perspective the enemy is still active, and who knows what they'll do to cause the Third Impact. Calling the MP Evas was the best course of action available. Not to mention that SEELE had an excuse to deploy them.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Fri Nov 19, 2010 6:32 pm Bagheera wrote: That would make sense if they showed up and speared her ass.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Nov 19, 2010 6:41 pm Allemann wrote: They did, only more ravaged than speared.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Nov 19, 2010 6:56 pm Bagheera wrote: None was given. Hence the thread.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Nov 19, 2010 7:06 pm Azathoth wrote: Yes, there was. Kiel complains that the Evas are getting in their way again and it's best to fight fire with fire, then sends out a bunch more Evas. Clearly he didn't feel that the JSSDF was doing a very good job, and I stand by what I said about it being impossible to rely on an Eva shutting down when its battery runs out. Look at it this way: if Kiel doesn't send the MPEs out there and Unit 02 goes berserk, they have no easy way of stopping it; it's going to kick the JSSDF's ass even more than it was already doing. If he does send the MPEs out there, he's got a pretty much unstoppable force on task. Also, for all he knows, Unit 01 could be poking its head into the Geofront at any time saying "oh hai guise whats going on in here" - even though Yui is, like Kiel, just waiting for Kyoko to get whacked, Kiel has no way of knowing that. He has to expect that Unit 02 is going to make trouble and he has to wipe it out with extreme prejudice.

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Fri Nov 19, 2010 9:06 pm Someone forgot to factor in the Kaworu Dummy Plugs. In Episode 24', Makoto told Misato that Kaworu can play merry hob with his sync rate at will. Misato (OFF): How did it go? Did you get any data on him? HYUGA: Here it is. I borrowed it from First Lieutenant Ibuki without her permission. Misato: I'm sorry I keep making you do these theft-like things. What is this?! HYUGA: It's no wonder Maya can't release this information. It's all theoretically impossible data. Misato: You're right. It only deepens the mystery. To think that he can freely set his synch rate with the Eva... And of his own will at that.

Her umbilical was severed, and both SEELE and the JSSDF knew this. Why bother with the Eva series? Why engage her at all instead of just letting her timer run out, pulling her out of Unit 02 and shooting her in the
